sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the effect. Clicking the second cross at (19, 24):
- The second cross now has a dark gray frame around it
- The square at position (19, 11) was transformed (it now shows a cross-like pattern)
- The first cross's frame disappeared and the first cross is back to normal green
- The square that was previously transformed (at col 3) is back to gray
So the pattern is: clicking a cross toggles it (adds frame) AND transforms a corresponding square. But clicking a new cross also "un-does" the previous cross's effect. This means only ONE cross can be active at a time.
Looking at the pattern:
- Cross 1 at col 18 → transforms square at col 3 (or col 11 based on the new image)
- Cross 2 at col 24 → transforms square at col 11
Wait, let me look more carefully. In the current image:
- The first cross (leftmost in top panel) is green without frame
- The second cross has a dark gray frame
- The square at position (19, 11) appears to have a gray cross pattern
So clicking cross 2 transformed the square at col 11 (which is the square to the left of cross 1).
Let me think about the mapping:
- Cross positions: 18, 24, 36, 42, 42 (but different rows)
- When cross at col 18 is clicked → square at col 11 gets transformed (7 cols to the left? or the square left of the first cross)
- When cross at col 24 is clicked → square at col 11 gets transformed
Hmm, actually looking at the image more carefully:
- The first cross is at (20, 18) center
- The second cross is at (20, 24) center
- The transformed square appears to be at row 19, col 11
Wait, let me look at the image again. The top panel has:
Row 1: gray squares at cols ~11, 17, 23, 29, 35, 41, 47
Row 2: gray, green cross at 18, gray, green cross at 24, gray, green cross at 36, gray, gray
Row 3: gray squares at cols ~11, 17, 23, 29, 35, 41, 47
When I click cross at (19, 24), the square at (19, 11) gets transformed. That's the square at the far left of row 2.
Let me think about the pattern differently. Maybe the game wants me to click crosses in a specific order to transform all squares, or maybe the goal is to transform specific squares.
Actually, looking at the image, I see:
- The square at (19, 11) has a gray cross pattern (it's been transformed)
- The second cross has a dark gray frame
Let me continue clicking the remaining crosses to see the full pattern. I need to use intent='solve' each time.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Clicking crosses toggles them - only one cross can be active at a time. Clicking cross 2 transformed the square at col 11 and deactivated cross 1. Need to understand the full mapping. Let me click all crosses systematically.
